texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Vitals/Measurements application is designed to store in the patient's electronic medical record all vital signs and various measurements associated with a patient's hospital stay or outpatient clinic visit.  Data can be accessed by several  (Veterans Health Information Systems and Technology Architecture) applications (e.g., CPRS, Health Summary) that interface with the Vitals/Measurements application.
texts.text: The Vitals/Measurements application is designed to store in the patient's electronic medical record all vital signs and various measurements associated with a patient's hospital stay or outpatient clinic visit.  Data can be accessed by several  (Veterans Health Information Systems and Technology Architecture) applications (e.g., CPRS, Health Summary) that interface with the Vitals/Measurements application.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: •	Contains a GUI (Graphical User Interface) to make editing and viewing of data easier.  Additional information on GUI software is contained at the end of this chapter.
texts.text: •	Contains a GUI (Graphical User Interface) to make editing and viewing of data easier.  Additional information on GUI software is contained at the end of this chapter.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: •	Supports documentation of a patient's vital signs (e.g., temperature, pulse, and respiration).
texts.text: •	Supports documentation of a patient's vital signs (e.g., temperature, pulse, and respiration).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: •	Tracks a patient's height, weight, central venous pressure (CVP), circumference/girth and oxygen saturation via oximetry with supplemental oxygen information.
texts.text: •	Tracks a patient's height, weight, central venous pressure (CVP), circumference/girth and oxygen saturation via oximetry with supplemental oxygen information.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: •	Supports documentation of detailed or positional blood pressures for a patient (i.e., bilateral blood pressures taken in a sitting, standing and lying position).

texts.orig: An “application window” is the area on your computer screen used by a program.  If you have more than one program running at the same time, you can go from one program to another by clicking in each application window. texts.orig: There is one security key in this application, it is GMV MANAGER.  This new key allows a user to view/create/edit all other user’s templates in the Vitals Manager module, without this key the user can only view/create/edit his/her own user templates.  This key also allows a user to use (run) other user’s templates in the Vitals application.  This key should be assigned to the package coordinator.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Some sites prefer to delay implementation of the software until they have a point of care data entry system, but this software can be implemented without a point of care system.  Vital sign entry can be accomplished by ancillary service personnel, (e.g., PIMS, Dietetics, Pharmacy).  Interested users of this software are encouraged to form a committee to work cooperatively on the implementation and training of the package.  Setting up test wards is a good way to begin a cooperative implementation effort.  The Vitals/Measurements module is appropriate for all personnel who obtain and record patient vitals/measurements.  Conceivably this module could be used by nursing, dietetics, medicine, and other disciplines as appropriate.

A number that represents the data grid background color for abnormal values. There are 16 colors to choose from.
0 - Black
1 - Maroon
2 - Green
3 - Olive
4 - Navy
5 - Purple
6 - Teal
7 - Gray
8 - Silver
9 - Red
10 - Lime
11 - Yellow
12 - Blue
13 - Fuchsia
14 - Aqua
15 – White
ON means the abnormal values appear in bold font. OFF means the abnormal values appear in regular font.
ON means the qualifiers are shown on the data grid for abnormal values. OFF means they are not shown for abnormal values.
The number that represents the text color for abnormal values shown in the data grid. (See the list above.)
An RPC returns an array with the list of clinics to select from. This number indicates the array node of the clinic selected. It is “-1” if no clinic was ever selected.
When the “Units as Drop Down List” checkbox in the “Enter Vitals” window is checked this value is “CPRSMetricStyle”. If it is not checked the value is “VitalsMetricStyle”.

User preferences for abnormal values. Set from File | User Options | Values Display tab.
1st value (e.g., 15) - Color of the background. Same as ABNORMALBGCOLOR.
2nd value (e.g., 9) - Color of the text. Same as ABNORMALTEXTCOLOR.
3rd value (e.g., 0) - Show text in bold font. Same as ABNORMALBOLD.
4th value (e.g., 1) - Show qualifiers. Same as ABNORMAL QUALIFIERS.
5th value (e.g. 15) - Background color for today’s values. Not currently used. User cannot set this value.

User preferences for normal values. Set from File | User Options | Values Display tab.
1st value (e.g., 15) - Color of the background. Same as NORMALBGCOLOR.
2nd value (e.g., 0) - Color of the text. Same as NORMALTEXTCOLOR. 
3rd value (e.g., 0) - Show text in bold font. Same as NORMALBOLD.
4th value (e.g., 1) - Show qualifiers. Same as NORMALQUALIFIERS.
5th value (e.g., 15) - Background color for today’s values. Not currently used. User cannot set this value.

The following values are set by the software. The user cannot change them. These values control the File | Patient Inquiry display. 
6th value (e.g., 15388544) – Background color
7th value (e.g., 15388544) – Text color
8th value (e.g., ORWPT PTINQ) – RPC Name
The input template selected by the user. In this example, the template belongs to user 547 in the NEW PERSON file (#200). The template name is “full list”.
1 means the “Values”, “Time Scale”, “3D” and “Allow Zoom” checkboxes are visible. 0 means they are hidden. This parameter is set from the Vitals Lite GUI.
The code for the background color for the data graph. There are too many colors to list.

The “Show/Hide Graph Option” controls a panel that contains the “Values”, “3D”, “Allow Zoom” and “Time Scale” checkboxes. ON means the panel will be displayed to the user. OFF means it will be hidden from view. 

Same as  the GRAPH OPTIONS VISIBLE parameter but is set from the Vitals Standalone GUI.
ON means the “Values” checkbox has a checkmark. OFF means it is blank.
ON means the “3D” checkbox has a checkmark. OFF means it is blank.
ON means the “Allow Zoom” checkbox has a checkmark. OFF means it is blank.
ON means the “Time Scale” checkbox has a checkmark. OFF means it is blank.

A number to indicate the vital type(s) that appear on the data graph. 
0 – Temperature
1 – Pulse
2 – Respiration
3 – B/P
4 – Pulse Ox
5 – Height
6 – Weight
7 – BMI
8 - Pain
9 – Height/Weight
10 - TPR
11 – B/P - Weight
12 – C/G
13 – CVP
14 – Intake
15 – Output
Width in pixels of the data grid.

selection. Default value is 12 (Two Years).
0 – Today
1 – T-1
2 – T-2
3 – T-3
4 – T-4
5 – T-5
6 – T-6
7 – T-7
8 – T-8
9 – T-9
10 – Six Months
11 – One Year
12 – Two Years
13 – All Results
14 –
Height in pixels of the “Latest vitals on file for this patient” portion of the “Enter Vitals’ window.
The background color for the normal values in the data grid. (See the list above.)
ON means the normal values appear in the bold font. OFF means the normal values appear in regular font.
ON means the qualifiers are shown on the data grid for normal values. OFF means they are not shown for normal values.
The number that represents the text color for normal values shown in the data grid. (See the list above.)
Not currently used. May be used in the future.
Width in pixels of the “Templates” portion of the “Enter Vitals” window.
ON means the “Enable R” checkbox in “Enter Vitals” window is checked. OFF means it is not checked.

A number that indicates which patient selector tab is selected.
0 – Unit
1 – Ward
2 – Team
3 – Clinic
4 – All

Indicates the number of seconds the software will wait before trying to automatically look up a patient name using the characters entered by the user. Set by the user in:
File | User Options | Search Delay tab.
Show or hide the values for the “Latest vitals on file for this patient” portion of the “Enter Vitals” window.
“ShowTemplates” means that the “Template” list in the “Enter Vitals” window is shown. “NoTemplates” means it is hidden.
An RPC returns an array with the list of teams to select from. This number indicates the array node of the team selected. It is “-1” if no team was ever selected.

Parameters of the Vitals Standalone (EXE) window.
1st value (e.g., 800)  – User’s Windows  screen resolution (width)
2nd value (e.g., 600) – User’s Windows screen resolution (height)
3rd value (e.g., -4) – Position of the left side of the window
4th value (e.g., -4) – Position of the top side of the window
5th value (e.g., 808) – Width in pixels of the window 
6th value (e.g., 574) – Height in pixels of the window
7th value (e.g., 0) – Not currently used. Cannot be set by the user.
An RPC returns an array with the list of (nursing) units to select from. This number indicates the array node of the unit selected. It is “-1” if no unit was ever selected.
ON means the “Enable U” checkbox in “Enter Vitals” window is checked. OFF means it is not checked.
The height in pixels for the Vitals Lite (DLL) View window.
The pixel location for the left side of the Vitals Lite (DLL).View window.
The pixel location for the top of the Vitals Lite (DLL) View window.
The width in pixels for the Viitals Lite (DLL) View window.

Parameters of the Vitals Lite (DLL) window.
1st value (e.g., 800)  – User’s Windows  screen resolution (width)
2nd value (e.g., 600) – User’s Windows screen resolution (height)
3rd value (e.g., 0) – Position of the left side of the window
4th value (e.g., 0) – Position of the top side of the window
5th value (e.g., 800) – Width in pixels of the window 
6th value (e.g., 566) – Height in pixels of the window
7th value (e.g., 0) – Not currently used. Cannot be set by the user.
An RPC returns an array with the list of wards to select from. This number indicates the array node of the ward selected. It is “-1” if no ward was ever selected.
